[Kaliiasseri](/wiki/Kalliasseri "Kalliasseri") * [Pappinisseri](/wiki/Pappinisseri "Pappinisseri") * [Azhikode North](/wiki/Azhikode_North "Azhikode North") * [Azhikode South](/wiki/Azhikode_So

is a [census town](/wiki/Census_town "Census town") near [Kuthuparamba](/wiki/Kuthuparamba "Kuthuparamba") in [Kannur district](/wiki/Kannur_district "Kannur district") of Kerala state in India. It is different from the city of [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam") in Kottayam district of the same state.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

Census Commission of India. Archived from [the original](http://www.censusindia.net/results/town.php?stad=A&state5=999) on 16 June 2004. Retrieved 1 November 2008.

**Kottayam-Malabar** is a [census town](/wiki/Census_town "Census town") near [Kuthuparamba](/wiki/Kuthuparamba "Kuthuparamba") in [Kannur district](/wiki/Kannur_district "Kannur district") of Kerala state in India. It is different from the city of [Kottayam](/wiki/Kottayam "Kottayam") in Kottayam district of the same state.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam-Malabar&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[Kingdom of Kottayam](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Kottayam "Kingdom of Kottayam") is a historic province of old Malabar district in India. It covered what is today Talassery Taluk of Kannur district. The headquarters of the kingdom was at Kottayam-Malabar town. The Raja of Kottayam presided over the province. Pazhassi Raja was a member of the western branch of the Kottayam royal clan. When [Hyder Ali](/wiki/Hyder_Ali "Hyder Ali") of the [Kingdom of Mysore](/wiki/Kingdom_of_Mysore "Kingdom of Mysore") [occupied Malabar](/wiki/Mysore_invasion_of_Kerala "Mysore invasion of Kerala") in 1773, the Raja of Kottayam found political asylum in Kallara near Vikom in Kottayam district of Kerala. Pazhassi Raja, the fourth prince in line for succession to the throne during this period, became one of the *de facto* heads of state, surpassing several older royal contenders.[[2]](#cite_note-Logan2000-2)[[3]](#cite_note-:0-3)

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam-Malabar&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Demographics")]

As of [2011 Census](/wiki/2011_Census_of_India "2011 Census of India"), Kottayam-Malabar [census town](/wiki/Census_town "Census town") had population of 19,176 which constitutes 8,813 males and 10,363 females. Kottayam-Malabar census town spreads over an area of 8.43 square kilometres (3.25 sq mi) with 3,815 families residing in it. The sex ratio was 1176 higher than state average of 1084. Population of children in the age group 0-6 was 2,302 (12%) where 1,141 are males and 1,161 are females. Kottayam-Malabar had an overall literacy of 96.2% higher than state average of 94%. The male literacy stands at 97.7% and female literacy was 95%.[[4]](#cite_note-4)

## Transportation

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kottayam-Malabar&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Transportation")]

The national highway passes through [Thalassery](/wiki/Thalassery "Thalassery") town. [Mangalore](/wiki/Mangalore "Mangalore"), Goa and [Mumbai](/wiki/Mumbai "Mumbai") can be accessed on the northern side and [Kochi](/wiki/Kochi "Kochi") and [Thiruvananthapuram](/wiki/Thiruvananthapuram "Thiruvananthapuram") can be accessed on the southern side. The road to the east of [Iritty](/wiki/Iritty "Iritty") connects to [Mysore](/wiki/Mysore "Mysore") and [Bangalore](/wiki/Bangalore "Bangalore"). The nearest railway station is [Thalassery](/wiki/Thalassery "Thalassery") on Mangalore-[Palakkad](/wiki/Palakkad "Palakkad") line.
Trains are available to almost all parts of India subject to advance booking over the internet. There are airports at Mattannur known as Kannur Airport,[Mangalore](/wiki/Mangalore_Airport "Mangalore Airport") and [Kozhikode](/wiki/Calicut_International_Airport "Calicut International Airport"). All of them are international airports but direct flights are available only to Middle Eastern countries.
